skill.name: qwen-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models through the inference.sh CLI, including text-to-image, multi-image editing, and text-heavy poster generation.
skill.summary: Use Qwen-Image-2.0 models from inference.sh for image generation and editing.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and network patterns. Most high-risk weak-crypto and Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown code spans, but the skill intentionally permits external CLI execution and remote inference requests. No malicious intent, credential theft, or prompt injection text was found in the reviewed skill file.

medium_findings.title: External Inference CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash execution for infsh and provides multiple infsh app run examples. This is expected for the skill, but it lets the agent invoke a local CLI that communicates with an external inference platform.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration explicitly grants Bash access for infsh, and the examples show direct infsh execution. The behavior is core functionality rather than hidden malware.

medium_findings.title: User Prompt and Image Data Sent to External Service
medium_findings.description: The documented workflow sends prompts and optional reference image URLs to inference.sh hosted apps. Users should treat submitted prompts and referenced images as data shared with a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The service URL and infsh app commands make remote inference data transfer clear. No evidence shows unauthorized exfiltration beyond the intended image-generation workflow.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby shell backtick alerts point to Markdown inline code, fenced command examples, and table entries. They are not executable Ruby code inside this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown, and the flagged backticks are formatting markers around examples and parameter names. There is no Ruby source file or dynamic shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm flags occur in descriptive Markdown text, model names, prompt examples, or table headings. No hashing or encryption implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations do not contain cryptographic code or security decisions. They are prose, examples, or table metadata in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Related Skill Installation Commands Require User Review
low_findings.description: The related-skills section suggests npx skills add commands. These are not within the declared infsh tool permission, but users should review any package installation before running it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation and not hidden execution. They still represent a supply-chain action if a user chooses to run them.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example References
low_findings.description: The URL alerts point to the inference.sh product site, documentation links, one image asset, and example.com reference image placeholders. No suspicious endpoint or secret-bearing request is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are transparent documentation or placeholder references. They do not show covert callbacks, encoded payloads, or credential transmission.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a Parameter Description False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance flag points to the negative_prompt parameter row. It describes image content to avoid and does not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a table row for an image-generation option. It contains no host commands, filesystem reads, or environment inspection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Bash Permission for infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools rule permits any infsh subcommand through Bash. This is necessary for flexible CLI use, but it broadens what the skill can ask the agent to execute.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard permission is explicitly present in the front matter. The risk is limited to infsh commands rather than arbitrary shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Image Reference Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The editing example accepts reference image URLs that may be fetched or processed by the inference service. Users should avoid private or sensitive image URLs unless policy allows it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reference_images array clearly contains remote URI inputs. The evidence supports external image processing, but not covert data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Design teams need fast image generation and editing without building model integrations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Qwen-Image-2.0 workflows using inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the infsh CLI.
Generates images from text prompts with optional size, count, seed, and negative prompt settings.
Edits images using one to three reference image URLs.
Supports prompt settings for text-heavy posters and precise rendered words.
Shows how to list related Qwen image apps and save sample input files.
limitations: Requires a working inference.sh CLI installation and account login.
Sends prompts and image references to an external inference service.
Does not include local model weights or offline image generation.
Generated quality depends on model availability, prompt detail, and service limits.

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate campaign images, posters, and social assets with controlled text placement and output dimensions.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Concepts
use_cases.description: Create visual concepts from short briefs before requesting final design work from a creative team.

use_cases.title: Edit Reference-Based Images
use_cases.description: Combine subject, clothing, pose, or style references into a new edited image using Qwen multi-image workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2 to generate one image of a clean product photo on a white background. Use 1024 by 1024 resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four visual directions for a modern coffee shop logo using Qwen-Image-2.0. Keep the style minimal and avoid complex backgrounds.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Text Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the Pro model for rendered words
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2-pro to create a poster with the title "Summer Sale" and subtitle "50% Off Everything". Disable prompt extension for precise text.

prompt_templates.title: Edit with References
prompt_templates.prompt: Use three reference image URLs to combine the person, outfit, and pose into one edited image. Preserve natural lighting and realistic proportions.

output_examples.input: A prompt asks for a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars.
output_examples.output: The skill prepares an inference.sh run for Qwen-Image-2.0 and returns generated PNG image results with dimensions metadata.

output_examples.input: A user requests a poster with exact headline and subtitle text.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the Pro model, uses explicit quoted text, and recommends disabling prompt extension for stronger text control.

output_examples.input: A user provides three reference image URLs for an edit.
output_examples.output: The skill structures a multi-reference editing request that describes how each source image should influence the result.

best_practices: Use the Pro model when the image must contain readable text.
Specify dimensions, style, lighting, and composition in the prompt.
Avoid sensitive prompts or private image URLs unless external processing is approved.
anti_patterns: Do not run installation commands from related-skill examples without reviewing the source.
Do not expect offline generation because the workflow depends on inference.sh.
Do not use vague prompts when exact text, layout, or brand style matters.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh CLI to run Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro apps.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents multi-reference editing with one to three reference image URLs.

faq.question: Can it render text inside images?
faq.answer: Yes. The Pro model is recommended for posters and other text-heavy designs.

faq.question: Does it send data outside my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts and reference image data are processed through the external inference.sh service.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is safe to publish with a warning about external CLI execution and third-party inference processing.
